<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kimi (kabane)]][[ja:キミ (カバネ)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kimi (kabane)]][[ja:キミ (カバネ)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kimi (kabane)]][[ja:キミ (カバネ)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q39078946]] Wikidata name: Kimi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q39078946]] Wikidata name: Kimi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Ancient Japanese hereditary title}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Italic title|reason=
}} {{nihongo|'''''Kimi'''''|君}} was an ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}ese hereditary title denoting Rank and political standing (a ''[[kabane]]'') that was reserved for certain members of the ''Tomo no [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Miyatsuko]]'' clans, which were clans associated with particular occupations. They became a {{ill|Hereditary title|en|Hereditary title|WD=Q5737899|lt=Hereditary title}} passed down the generations. The ''kimi'' Rank was lower than the ''[[muraji]]'' and ''[[Omi (title)|omi]]'' Ranks in political power and standing during much of the [[Kofun period]] and {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} which are collectively grouped as the {{ill|Yamato period|en|Yamato period|WD=Q633881|lt=Yamato period}}.<ref>Nihon Shoki</ref><ref>Shinsen Shōjiroku</ref>
"''Kimi''" literally means "Lord" (of a local area). For example, in the name, Kamitsukeno no Kimi Wakako, "Kamitsukeno no kimi" means "Lord of Kamitsukeno" and Wakako is his personal name. Sometimes the character (公) is used in place of (君).<ref name="Clans and Religion in Ancient Japan">{{cite book|last1=Suzuki|first1=Masanobu|title=Clans and Religion in Ancient Japan: The mythology of Mt. Miwa|date=2016|publisher=Taylor & Francis|location=Routledge|isbn=9781317209348|pages=170|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=h0InDAAAQBAJ|accessdate=1 August 2017}}</ref>
==List of ''Kimi'' (君)== ** '''Oka no Kimi Yoroshi''' (岡君宜), sent as envoy to {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty}}, {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}} for [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. * {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province}} (豊後国) ** '''Okita no Kimi Esaka''' (大分君惠尺, died 675), important member of the court for {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}}. ** '''Okita no Kimi Wakaomi''' (大分君稚臣, died 679) also called '''Wakami''' (稚見), important member of the court for {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}}. * {{ill|Hi Province|en|Hi Province|WD=Q1156510|lt=Hi Province}} (火国 or 肥国) ** '''Hi no Kimi Take Wokumi''' (火君健男組 / 肥君健男組), legendary ancestor of the Hi no Kimi clan who was granted the title by {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}} for pacifying the area. ** '''Hi no Kimi Ite''' (火君猪手 / 肥君猪手), lived in northern Kyushu in 702 during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu}}. * {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province}} (上野国) ** '''Sami no Sukunamaro''' (佐味宿那麻呂 / 佐味君少麻呂), fought for [[:en:Emperor Temmu|Emperor Temmu]] during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}}. * {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province}} (美濃国) ** '''Mori no Kimi Oiwa''' (守君大石), sent in 661 to rescue [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] by {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} who sent him after in 665 to the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty}} as an envoy. ** '''Mori no Kimi Karita''' (守君刈田), granted the Rank of Jikiko-shi by {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}} in 691. * {{ill|Nagato Province|en|Nagato Province|WD=Q907382|lt=Nagato Province}} (長門国) ** '''Ina no Kimi Takami''' (猪名公高見, died 672), lived during the time of [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. * {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province}} (近江国) ** '''Inukami no Kimi Mitasuki''' (犬上君三田耜 / 犬上御田鍬), sent by {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} in 614 to {{ill|Sui dynasty|en|Sui dynasty|WD=Q7405|lt=Sui dynasty}}, {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}} returning home 615 from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]]. Also sent by {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei}} as an envoy to the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty}}, {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}} in 630. ** '''Inukami no Kimi Takerube''' (犬上君健部), minister of [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. ** '''Inukami no Kimi Shiromaro''' (犬上君白麻呂), son of Mitasuki, sent as ambassador by [[:en:Empress Saimei|Empress Saimei]] to {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}. * {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}} (下野国) ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Okuma''' (上毛野君小熊), participated in the feud of the [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}}, Omi and his relative Oki. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Katana''' (上毛野君形名), father of Wakako. Also sent by {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei}} as an envoy to the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty}}, {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}} in 630. ** '''Inukami no Kimi Takerube''' (犬上君健部), minister of [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. ** '''Inukami no Kimi Shiromaro''' (犬上君白麻呂), son of Mitasuki, sent as ambassador by [[:en:Empress Saimei|Empress Saimei]] to {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}. * {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}} (下野国) ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Okuma''' (上毛野君小熊), participated in the feud of the [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}}, Omi and his relative Oki. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Katana''' (上毛野君形名), father of Wakako. In 637 he led an expedition against the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}}. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Wakako''' (上毛野君稚子), led 27,000 soldiers to help [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] in 662. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Michiji''' (上毛野君三千), son of Wakako, government official. * {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province}} (駿河国) ** '''Iohara no Kimi''' (廬原君), led 10,000 soldiers to help [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] in 662. Personal name unknown. * {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|WD=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province}} (筑紫国) ** '''{{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Tsukushi no Kimi Iwai}}''' (筑紫君磐井, died 528), political arch-rival of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}} and led a failed rebellion in 527, known as the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Iwai Rebellion}} ending with his death. The Iwatoyama kofun in {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka}} was recorded in ''Fudoki of Chikugo'' to be his tomb. ** '''Tsukushi no Kimi Kazuko''' (筑紫君葛子), son of Iwai. He feared being put to death on implication because of his father's rebellion, and gave to the Emperor the ''miyake'' of Kasuya. There is no record of his death, so it is believed he lived on. ** '''Tsukushi no Kimi Sachiyama''' (筑紫君薩野馬 / 筑紫君薩夜麻), in 671 he served as an envoy for {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} and in 690 for [[:en:Empress Jito|Empress Jito]] with the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty}} of {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}}. * {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province}} (Yamashiro Province ) ** '''Miwa no Kimi Sakau''' (三輪君逆, died 586), brother of Osazaki. He was minister for [[Emperor Bidatsu]]. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Osazaki''' (三輪君小鷦鷯), brother of Sakau. He suffered intense questioning for raping the ladies in waiting of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei}} and stabbed himself in the neck and died. ** '''Miwa no Kurukuma no Kimi Azumahito''' (三輪栗隈君東人), from a cadet branch of the Miwa clan. In 645 he was dispatched two times to confirm the border between [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] and the [[:en:Gaya confederacy|Gaya confederacy]]. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Okuchi''' (輪君大口), from a cadet branch of the Miwa clan. Lived during the reign of [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Fumiya''' (三輪文屋, died 643), son of Osazaki. He escaped with {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|en|Prince Yamashiro|WD=Q3137695|lt=Prince Yamashiro}}, the son of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku}} to {{ill|Mount Ikoma|en|Mount Ikoma|WD=Q996259|lt=Mount Ikoma}} (生駒山) and they were both killed there. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Shikobu''' (三輪君色夫), son of Sakau. He was dispatched with the army to {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}} in 649. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Nemaro''' (三輪君根麻呂), general who was dispatched to Japan with 27,000 soldiers in 663 and fought the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|en|Battle of Baekgang|WD=Q608727|lt=Battle of Baekgang}}. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Togane''' (三輪君利金), son of Fumiya, father of Takechimaro. He was Ranked Daikajō (大花上), which was a high crown Rank. The Ōmiwa clan regained power during his generation. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Kobito''' (三輪君子首, died 676), son of Shikobu. He was minister of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}}. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Takechimaro''' (三輪君高市麻呂, 657–706), son of Togane, grandson of Fumiya. He fought for {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}}.
==See also== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} * [[Gōzoku]] * [[Kabane]] * [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] * [[Omi (title)|Omi]] * [[Muraji]] * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} * {{ill|Yamato period|en|Yamato period|WD=Q633881|lt=Yamato period}}
== Notes == {{reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kimi (kabane)]][[ja:キミ (カバネ)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kimi (kabane)]][[ja:キミ (カバネ)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kimi (kabane)]][[ja:キミ (カバネ)]]
{{Short description|Ancient Japanese hereditary title}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Italic title|reason=
}} {{nihongo|'''''Kimi'''''|君}} was an ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}ese hereditary title denoting Rank and political standing (a ''[[kabane]]'') that was reserved for certain members of the ''Tomo no [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Miyatsuko]]'' clans, which were clans associated with particular occupations. They became a {{ill|Hereditary title|en|Hereditary title|WD=Q5737899|lt=Hereditary title}} passed down the generations. The ''kimi'' Rank was lower than the ''[[muraji]]'' and ''[[Omi (title)|omi]]'' Ranks in political power and standing during much of the [[Kofun period]] and {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} which are collectively grouped as the {{ill|Yamato period|en|Yamato period|WD=Q633881|lt=Yamato period}}.<ref>Nihon Shoki</ref><ref>Shinsen Shōjiroku</ref>
"''Kimi''" literally means "Lord" (of a local area). For example, in the name, Kamitsukeno no Kimi Wakako, "Kamitsukeno no kimi" means "Lord of Kamitsukeno" and Wakako is his personal name. Sometimes the character (公) is used in place of (君).<ref name="Clans and Religion in Ancient Japan">{{cite book|last1=Suzuki|first1=Masanobu|title=Clans and Religion in Ancient Japan: The mythology of Mt. Miwa|date=2016|publisher=Taylor & Francis|location=Routledge|isbn=9781317209348|pages=170|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=h0InDAAAQBAJ|accessdate=1 August 2017}}</ref>
==List of ''Kimi'' (君)== ** '''Oka no Kimi Yoroshi''' (岡君宜), sent as envoy to {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty}}, {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}} for [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. * {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province}} (豊後国) ** '''Okita no Kimi Esaka''' (大分君惠尺, died 675), important member of the court for {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}}. ** '''Okita no Kimi Wakaomi''' (大分君稚臣, died 679) also called '''Wakami''' (稚見), important member of the court for {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}}. * {{ill|Hi Province|en|Hi Province|WD=Q1156510|lt=Hi Province}} (火国 or 肥国) ** '''Hi no Kimi Take Wokumi''' (火君健男組 / 肥君健男組), legendary ancestor of the Hi no Kimi clan who was granted the title by {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}} for pacifying the area. ** '''Hi no Kimi Ite''' (火君猪手 / 肥君猪手), lived in northern Kyushu in 702 during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu}}. * {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province}} (上野国) ** '''Sami no Sukunamaro''' (佐味宿那麻呂 / 佐味君少麻呂), fought for [[:en:Emperor Temmu|Emperor Temmu]] during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}}. * {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province}} (美濃国) ** '''Mori no Kimi Oiwa''' (守君大石), sent in 661 to rescue [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] by {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} who sent him after in 665 to the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty}} as an envoy. ** '''Mori no Kimi Karita''' (守君刈田), granted the Rank of Jikiko-shi by {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}} in 691. * {{ill|Nagato Province|en|Nagato Province|WD=Q907382|lt=Nagato Province}} (長門国) ** '''Ina no Kimi Takami''' (猪名公高見, died 672), lived during the time of [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. * {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province}} (近江国) ** '''Inukami no Kimi Mitasuki''' (犬上君三田耜 / 犬上御田鍬), sent by {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} in 614 to {{ill|Sui dynasty|en|Sui dynasty|WD=Q7405|lt=Sui dynasty}}, {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}} returning home 615 from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]]. Also sent by {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei}} as an envoy to the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty}}, {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}} in 630. ** '''Inukami no Kimi Takerube''' (犬上君健部), minister of [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. ** '''Inukami no Kimi Shiromaro''' (犬上君白麻呂), son of Mitasuki, sent as ambassador by [[:en:Empress Saimei|Empress Saimei]] to {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}. * {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}} (下野国) ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Okuma''' (上毛野君小熊), participated in the feud of the [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}}, Omi and his relative Oki. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Katana''' (上毛野君形名), father of Wakako. In 637 he led an expedition against the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}}. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Wakako''' (上毛野君稚子), led 27,000 soldiers to help [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] in 662. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Michiji''' (上毛野君三千), son of Wakako, government official. * {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province}} (駿河国) ** '''Iohara no Kimi''' (廬原君), led 10,000 soldiers to help [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] in 662. Personal name unknown. * {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|WD=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province}} (筑紫国) ** '''{{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Tsukushi no Kimi Iwai}}''' (筑紫君磐井, died 528), political arch-rival of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}} and led a failed rebellion in 527, known as the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Iwai Rebellion}} ending with his death. The Iwatoyama kofun in {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka}} was recorded in ''Fudoki of Chikugo'' to be his tomb. ** '''Tsukushi no Kimi Kazuko''' (筑紫君葛子), son of Iwai. He feared being put to death on implication because of his father's rebellion, and gave to the Emperor the ''miyake'' of Kasuya. There is no record of his death, so it is believed he lived on. ** '''Tsukushi no Kimi Sachiyama''' (筑紫君薩野馬 / 筑紫君薩夜麻), in 671 he served as an envoy for {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} and in 690 for [[:en:Empress Jito|Empress Jito]] with the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty}} of {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}}. * {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province}} (Yamashiro Province ) ** '''Miwa no Kimi Sakau''' (三輪君逆, died 586), brother of Osazaki. He was minister for [[Emperor Bidatsu]]. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Osazaki''' (三輪君小鷦鷯), brother of Sakau. He suffered intense questioning for raping the ladies in waiting of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei}} and stabbed himself in the neck and died. ** '''Miwa no Kurukuma no Kimi Azumahito''' (三輪栗隈君東人), from a cadet branch of the Miwa clan. In 645 he was dispatched two times to confirm the border between [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] and the [[:en:Gaya confederacy|Gaya confederacy]]. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Okuchi''' (輪君大口), from a cadet branch of the Miwa clan. Lived during the reign of [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Fumiya''' (三輪文屋, died 643), son of Osazaki. He escaped with {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|en|Prince Yamashiro|WD=Q3137695|lt=Prince Yamashiro}}, the son of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku}} to {{ill|Mount Ikoma|en|Mount Ikoma|WD=Q996259|lt=Mount Ikoma}} (生駒山) and they were both killed there. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Shikobu''' (三輪君色夫), son of Sakau. He was dispatched with the army to {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}} in 649. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Nemaro''' (三輪君根麻呂), general who was dispatched to Japan with 27,000 soldiers in 663 and fought the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|en|Battle of Baekgang|WD=Q608727|lt=Battle of Baekgang}}. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Togane''' (三輪君利金), son of Fumiya, father of Takechimaro. He was Ranked Daikajō (大花上), which was a high crown Rank. The Ōmiwa clan regained power during his generation. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Kobito''' (三輪君子首, died 676), son of Shikobu. He was minister of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}}. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Takechimaro''' (三輪君高市麻呂, 657–706), son of Togane, grandson of Fumiya. He fought for {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}}.

Also sent by {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei}} as an envoy to the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty}}, {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}} in 630. ** '''Inukami no Kimi Takerube''' (犬上君健部), minister of [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. ** '''Inukami no Kimi Shiromaro''' (犬上君白麻呂), son of Mitasuki, sent as ambassador by [[:en:Empress Saimei|Empress Saimei]] to {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo}}. * {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}} (下野国) ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Okuma''' (上毛野君小熊), participated in the feud of the [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}}, Omi and his relative Oki. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Katana''' (上毛野君形名), father of Wakako. In 637 he led an expedition against the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}}. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Wakako''' (上毛野君稚子), led 27,000 soldiers to help [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] in 662. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Michiji''' (上毛野君三千), son of Wakako, government official. * {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province}} (駿河国) ** '''Iohara no Kimi''' (廬原君), led 10,000 soldiers to help [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] in 662. Personal name unknown. * {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|WD=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province}} (筑紫国) ** '''{{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Tsukushi no Kimi Iwai}}''' (筑紫君磐井, died 528), political arch-rival of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai}} and led a failed rebellion in 527, known as the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Iwai Rebellion}} ending with his death. The Iwatoyama kofun in {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka}} was recorded in ''Fudoki of Chikugo'' to be his tomb. ** '''Tsukushi no Kimi Kazuko''' (筑紫君葛子), son of Iwai. He feared being put to death on implication because of his father's rebellion, and gave to the Emperor the ''miyake'' of Kasuya. There is no record of his death, so it is believed he lived on. ** '''Tsukushi no Kimi Sachiyama''' (筑紫君薩野馬 / 筑紫君薩夜麻), in 671 he served as an envoy for {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}} and in 690 for [[:en:Empress Jito|Empress Jito]] with the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty}} of {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}}. * {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province}} (Yamashiro Province ) ** '''Miwa no Kimi Sakau''' (三輪君逆, died 586), brother of Osazaki. He was minister for [[Emperor Bidatsu]]. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Osazaki''' (三輪君小鷦鷯), brother of Sakau. He suffered intense questioning for raping the ladies in waiting of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei}} and stabbed himself in the neck and died. ** '''Miwa no Kurukuma no Kimi Azumahito''' (三輪栗隈君東人), from a cadet branch of the Miwa clan. In 645 he was dispatched two times to confirm the border between [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] and the [[:en:Gaya confederacy|Gaya confederacy]]. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Okuchi''' (輪君大口), from a cadet branch of the Miwa clan. Lived during the reign of [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Fumiya''' (三輪文屋, died 643), son of Osazaki. He escaped with {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|en|Prince Yamashiro|WD=Q3137695|lt=Prince Yamashiro}}, the son of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku}} to {{ill|Mount Ikoma|en|Mount Ikoma|WD=Q996259|lt=Mount Ikoma}} (生駒山) and they were both killed there. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Shikobu''' (三輪君色夫), son of Sakau. He was dispatched with the army to {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}} in 649. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Nemaro''' (三輪君根麻呂), general who was dispatched to Japan with 27,000 soldiers in 663 and fought the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|en|Battle of Baekgang|WD=Q608727|lt=Battle of Baekgang}}. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Togane''' (三輪君利金), son of Fumiya, father of Takechimaro. He was Ranked Daikajō (大花上), which was a high crown Rank. The Ōmiwa clan regained power during his generation. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Kobito''' (三輪君子首, died 676), son of Shikobu. He was minister of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}}. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Takechimaro''' (三輪君高市麻呂, 657–706), son of Togane, grandson of Fumiya. He fought for {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu}} during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War}}.
==See also== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} * [[Gōzoku]] * [[Kabane]] * [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] * [[Omi (title)|Omi]] * [[Muraji]] * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} * {{ill|Yamato period|en|Yamato period|WD=Q633881|lt=Yamato period}}
== Notes == {{reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kimi (kabane)]][[ja:キミ (カバネ)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kimi (kabane)]][[ja:キミ (カバネ)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kimi (kabane)]][[ja:キミ (カバネ)]]
{{Short description|Ancient Japanese hereditary title}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Italic title|reason=
}} {{nihongo|'''''Kimi'''''|君}} was an ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}ese hereditary title denoting Rank and political standing (a ''[[kabane]]'') that was reserved for certain members of the ''Tomo no [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Miyatsuko]]'' clans, which were clans associated with particular occupations. They became a {{ill|Hereditary title|en|Hereditary title|WD=Q5737899|lt=Hereditary title|1=Hereditary title}} passed down the generations. The ''kimi'' Rank was lower than the ''[[muraji]]'' and ''[[Omi (title)|omi]]'' Ranks in political power and standing during much of the [[Kofun period]] and {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} which are collectively grouped as the {{ill|Yamato period|en|Yamato period|WD=Q633881|lt=Yamato period|1=Yamato period}}.<ref>Nihon Shoki</ref><ref>Shinsen Shōjiroku</ref>
"''Kimi''" literally means "Lord" (of a local area). For example, in the name, Kamitsukeno no Kimi Wakako, "Kamitsukeno no kimi" means "Lord of Kamitsukeno" and Wakako is his personal name. Sometimes the character (公) is used in place of (君).<ref name="Clans and Religion in Ancient Japan">{{cite book|last1=Suzuki|first1=Masanobu|title=Clans and Religion in Ancient Japan: The mythology of Mt. Miwa|date=2016|publisher=Taylor & Francis|location=Routledge|isbn=9781317209348|pages=170|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=h0InDAAAQBAJ|accessdate=1 August 2017}}</ref>
==List of ''Kimi'' (君)== ** '''Oka no Kimi Yoroshi''' (岡君宜), sent as envoy to {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty|1=Tang dynasty}}, {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China|1=China}} for [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. * {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province|1=Bungo Province}} (豊後国) ** '''Okita no Kimi Esaka''' (大分君惠尺, died 675), important member of the court for {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}. ** '''Okita no Kimi Wakaomi''' (大分君稚臣, died 679) also called '''Wakami''' (稚見), important member of the court for {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}. * {{ill|Hi Province|en|Hi Province|WD=Q1156510|lt=Hi Province|1=Hi Province}} (火国 or 肥国) ** '''Hi no Kimi Take Wokumi''' (火君健男組 / 肥君健男組), legendary ancestor of the Hi no Kimi clan who was granted the title by {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}} for pacifying the area. ** '''Hi no Kimi Ite''' (火君猪手 / 肥君猪手), lived in northern Kyushu in 702 during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu|1=Emperor Monmu}}. * {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province|1=Kōzuke Province}} (上野国) ** '''Sami no Sukunamaro''' (佐味宿那麻呂 / 佐味君少麻呂), fought for [[:en:Emperor Temmu|Emperor Temmu]] during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}. * {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}} (美濃国) ** '''Mori no Kimi Oiwa''' (守君大石), sent in 661 to rescue [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] by {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} who sent him after in 665 to the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty|1=Tang dynasty}} as an envoy. ** '''Mori no Kimi Karita''' (守君刈田), granted the Rank of Jikiko-shi by {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}} in 691. * {{ill|Nagato Province|en|Nagato Province|WD=Q907382|lt=Nagato Province|1=Nagato Province}} (長門国) ** '''Ina no Kimi Takami''' (猪名公高見, died 672), lived during the time of [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. * {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}} (近江国) ** '''Inukami no Kimi Mitasuki''' (犬上君三田耜 / 犬上御田鍬), sent by {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} in 614 to {{ill|Sui dynasty|en|Sui dynasty|WD=Q7405|lt=Sui dynasty|1=Sui dynasty}}, {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China|1=China}} returning home 615 from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]]. Also sent by {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}} as an envoy to the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty|1=Tang dynasty}}, {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China|1=China}} in 630. ** '''Inukami no Kimi Takerube''' (犬上君健部), minister of [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. ** '''Inukami no Kimi Shiromaro''' (犬上君白麻呂), son of Mitasuki, sent as ambassador by [[:en:Empress Saimei|Empress Saimei]] to {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}}. * {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}} (下野国) ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Okuma''' (上毛野君小熊), participated in the feud of the [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}, Omi and his relative Oki. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Katana''' (上毛野君形名), father of Wakako. In 637 he led an expedition against the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Wakako''' (上毛野君稚子), led 27,000 soldiers to help [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] in 662. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Michiji''' (上毛野君三千), son of Wakako, government official. * {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province|1=Suruga Province}} (駿河国) ** '''Iohara no Kimi''' (廬原君), led 10,000 soldiers to help [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] in 662. Personal name unknown. * {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|WD=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province|1=Tsukushi Province}} (筑紫国) ** '''{{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Tsukushi no Kimi Iwai|1=Iwai Rebellion}}''' (筑紫君磐井, died 528), political arch-rival of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} and led a failed rebellion in 527, known as the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Iwai Rebellion|1=Iwai Rebellion}} ending with his death. The Iwatoyama kofun in {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka|1=Fukuoka}} was recorded in ''Fudoki of Chikugo'' to be his tomb. ** '''Tsukushi no Kimi Kazuko''' (筑紫君葛子), son of Iwai. He feared being put to death on implication because of his father's rebellion, and gave to the Emperor the ''miyake'' of Kasuya. There is no record of his death, so it is believed he lived on. ** '''Tsukushi no Kimi Sachiyama''' (筑紫君薩野馬 / 筑紫君薩夜麻), in 671 he served as an envoy for {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} and in 690 for [[:en:Empress Jito|Empress Jito]] with the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty|1=Tang dynasty}} of {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China|1=China}}. * {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}} (Yamashiro Province ) ** '''Miwa no Kimi Sakau''' (三輪君逆, died 586), brother of Osazaki. He was minister for [[Emperor Bidatsu]]. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Osazaki''' (三輪君小鷦鷯), brother of Sakau. He suffered intense questioning for raping the ladies in waiting of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}} and stabbed himself in the neck and died. ** '''Miwa no Kurukuma no Kimi Azumahito''' (三輪栗隈君東人), from a cadet branch of the Miwa clan. In 645 he was dispatched two times to confirm the border between [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] and the [[:en:Gaya confederacy|Gaya confederacy]]. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Okuchi''' (輪君大口), from a cadet branch of the Miwa clan. Lived during the reign of [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Fumiya''' (三輪文屋, died 643), son of Osazaki. He escaped with {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|en|Prince Yamashiro|WD=Q3137695|lt=Prince Yamashiro|1=Prince Yamashiro}}, the son of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}} to {{ill|Mount Ikoma|en|Mount Ikoma|WD=Q996259|lt=Mount Ikoma|1=Mount Ikoma}} (生駒山) and they were both killed there. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Shikobu''' (三輪君色夫), son of Sakau. He was dispatched with the army to {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}} in 649. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Nemaro''' (三輪君根麻呂), general who was dispatched to Japan with 27,000 soldiers in 663 and fought the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|en|Battle of Baekgang|WD=Q608727|lt=Battle of Baekgang|1=Battle of Baekgang}}. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Togane''' (三輪君利金), son of Fumiya, father of Takechimaro. He was Ranked Daikajō (大花上), which was a high crown Rank. The Ōmiwa clan regained power during his generation. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Kobito''' (三輪君子首, died 676), son of Shikobu. He was minister of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Takechimaro''' (三輪君高市麻呂, 657–706), son of Togane, grandson of Fumiya. He fought for {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}.

(犬上君三田耜 / 犬上御田鍬), sent by {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} in 614 to {{ill|Sui dynasty|en|Sui dynasty|WD=Q7405|lt=Sui dynasty|1=Sui dynasty}}, {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China|1=China}} returning home 615 from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]]. Also sent by {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}} as an envoy to the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty|1=Tang dynasty}}, {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China|1=China}} in 630. ** '''Inukami no Kimi Takerube''' (犬上君健部), minister of [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. ** '''Inukami no Kimi Shiromaro''' (犬上君白麻呂), son of Mitasuki, sent as ambassador by [[:en:Empress Saimei|Empress Saimei]] to {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}}. * {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}} (下野国) ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Okuma''' (上毛野君小熊), participated in the feud of the [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}, Omi and his relative Oki. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Katana''' (上毛野君形名), father of Wakako. In 637 he led an expedition against the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Wakako''' (上毛野君稚子), led 27,000 soldiers to help [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] in 662. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Michiji''' (上毛野君三千), son of Wakako, government official. * {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province|1=Suruga Province}} (駿河国) ** '''Iohara no Kimi''' (廬原君), led 10,000 soldiers to help [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] in 662. Personal name unknown. * {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|WD=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province|1=Tsukushi Province}} (筑紫国) ** '''{{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Tsukushi no Kimi Iwai|1=Iwai Rebellion}}''' (筑紫君磐井, died 528), political arch-rival of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} and led a failed rebellion in 527, known as the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Iwai Rebellion|1=Iwai Rebellion}} ending with his death. The Iwatoyama kofun in {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka|1=Fukuoka}} was recorded in ''Fudoki of Chikugo'' to be his tomb. ** '''Tsukushi no Kimi Kazuko''' (筑紫君葛子), son of Iwai. He feared being put to death on implication because of his father's rebellion, and gave to the Emperor the ''miyake'' of Kasuya. There is no record of his death, so it is believed he lived on. ** '''Tsukushi no Kimi Sachiyama''' (筑紫君薩野馬 / 筑紫君薩夜麻), in 671 he served as an envoy for {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} and in 690 for [[:en:Empress Jito|Empress Jito]] with the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty|1=Tang dynasty}} of {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China|1=China}}. * {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}} (Yamashiro Province ) ** '''Miwa no Kimi Sakau''' (三輪君逆, died 586), brother of Osazaki. He was minister for [[Emperor Bidatsu]]. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Osazaki''' (三輪君小鷦鷯), brother of Sakau. He suffered intense questioning for raping the ladies in waiting of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}} and stabbed himself in the neck and died. ** '''Miwa no Kurukuma no Kimi Azumahito''' (三輪栗隈君東人), from a cadet branch of the Miwa clan. In 645 he was dispatched two times to confirm the border between [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] and the [[:en:Gaya confederacy|Gaya confederacy]]. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Okuchi''' (輪君大口), from a cadet branch of the Miwa clan. Lived during the reign of [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Fumiya''' (三輪文屋, died 643), son of Osazaki. He escaped with {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|en|Prince Yamashiro|WD=Q3137695|lt=Prince Yamashiro|1=Prince Yamashiro}}, the son of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}} to {{ill|Mount Ikoma|en|Mount Ikoma|WD=Q996259|lt=Mount Ikoma|1=Mount Ikoma}} (生駒山) and they were both killed there. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Shikobu''' (三輪君色夫), son of Sakau. He was dispatched with the army to {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}} in 649. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Nemaro''' (三輪君根麻呂), general who was dispatched to Japan with 27,000 soldiers in 663 and fought the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|en|Battle of Baekgang|WD=Q608727|lt=Battle of Baekgang|1=Battle of Baekgang}}. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Togane''' (三輪君利金), son of Fumiya, father of Takechimaro. He was Ranked Daikajō (大花上), which was a high crown Rank. The Ōmiwa clan regained power during his generation. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Kobito''' (三輪君子首, died 676), son of Shikobu. He was minister of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Takechimaro''' (三輪君高市麻呂, 657–706), son of Togane, grandson of Fumiya. He fought for {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}.
==See also== {{Portal|Ancient Japan}} * [[Gōzoku]] * [[Kabane]] * [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] * [[Omi (title)|Omi]] * [[Muraji]] * [[Kofun period]] * {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} * {{ill|Yamato period|en|Yamato period|WD=Q633881|lt=Yamato period|1=Yamato period}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kimi (kabane)]][[ja:キミ (カバネ)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kimi (kabane)]][[ja:キミ (カバネ)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kimi (kabane)]][[ja:キミ (カバネ)]]
{{Short description|Ancient Japanese hereditary title}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Italic title|reason=
}} {{nihongo|'''''Kimi'''''|君}} was an ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}ese hereditary title denoting Rank and political standing (a ''[[kabane]]'') that was reserved for certain members of the ''Tomo no [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Miyatsuko]]'' clans, which were clans associated with particular occupations. They became a {{ill|Hereditary title|en|Hereditary title|WD=Q5737899|lt=Hereditary title|1=Hereditary title}} passed down the generations. The ''kimi'' Rank was lower than the ''[[muraji]]'' and ''[[Omi (title)|omi]]'' Ranks in political power and standing during much of the [[Kofun period]] and {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} which are collectively grouped as the {{ill|Yamato period|en|Yamato period|WD=Q633881|lt=Yamato period|1=Yamato period}}.<ref>Nihon Shoki</ref><ref>Shinsen Shōjiroku</ref>
"''Kimi''" literally means "Lord" (of a local area). For example, in the name, Kamitsukeno no Kimi Wakako, "Kamitsukeno no kimi" means "Lord of Kamitsukeno" and Wakako is his personal name. Sometimes the character (公) is used in place of (君).<ref name="Clans and Religion in Ancient Japan">{{cite book|last1=Suzuki|first1=Masanobu|title=Clans and Religion in Ancient Japan: The mythology of Mt. Miwa|date=2016|publisher=Taylor & Francis|location=Routledge|isbn=9781317209348|pages=170|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=h0InDAAAQBAJ|accessdate=1 August 2017}}</ref>
==List of ''Kimi'' (君)== ** '''Oka no Kimi Yoroshi''' (岡君宜), sent as envoy to {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty|1=Tang dynasty}}, {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China|1=China}} for [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. * {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province|1=Bungo Province}} (豊後国) ** '''Okita no Kimi Esaka''' (大分君惠尺, died 675), important member of the court for {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}. ** '''Okita no Kimi Wakaomi''' (大分君稚臣, died 679) also called '''Wakami''' (稚見), important member of the court for {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}. * {{ill|Hi Province|en|Hi Province|WD=Q1156510|lt=Hi Province|1=Hi Province}} (火国 or 肥国) ** '''Hi no Kimi Take Wokumi''' (火君健男組 / 肥君健男組), legendary ancestor of the Hi no Kimi clan who was granted the title by {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}} for pacifying the area. ** '''Hi no Kimi Ite''' (火君猪手 / 肥君猪手), lived in northern Kyushu in 702 during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|WD=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu|1=Emperor Monmu}}. * {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province|1=Kōzuke Province}} (上野国) ** '''Sami no Sukunamaro''' (佐味宿那麻呂 / 佐味君少麻呂), fought for [[:en:Emperor Temmu|Emperor Temmu]] during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}. * {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}} (美濃国) ** '''Mori no Kimi Oiwa''' (守君大石), sent in 661 to rescue [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] by {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} who sent him after in 665 to the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty|1=Tang dynasty}} as an envoy. ** '''Mori no Kimi Karita''' (守君刈田), granted the Rank of Jikiko-shi by {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}} in 691. * {{ill|Nagato Province|en|Nagato Province|WD=Q907382|lt=Nagato Province|1=Nagato Province}} (長門国) ** '''Ina no Kimi Takami''' (猪名公高見, died 672), lived during the time of [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. * {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}} (近江国) ** '''Inukami no Kimi Mitasuki''' (犬上君三田耜 / 犬上御田鍬), sent by {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} in 614 to {{ill|Sui dynasty|en|Sui dynasty|WD=Q7405|lt=Sui dynasty|1=Sui dynasty}}, {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China|1=China}} returning home 615 from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]]. Also sent by {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}} as an envoy to the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty|1=Tang dynasty}}, {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China|1=China}} in 630. ** '''Inukami no Kimi Takerube''' (犬上君健部), minister of [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. ** '''Inukami no Kimi Shiromaro''' (犬上君白麻呂), son of Mitasuki, sent as ambassador by [[:en:Empress Saimei|Empress Saimei]] to {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}}. * {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}} (下野国) ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Okuma''' (上毛野君小熊), participated in the feud of the [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}, Omi and his relative Oki. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Katana''' (上毛野君形名), father of Wakako. In 637 he led an expedition against the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Wakako''' (上毛野君稚子), led 27,000 soldiers to help [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] in 662. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Michiji''' (上毛野君三千), son of Wakako, government official. * {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province|1=Suruga Province}} (駿河国) ** '''Iohara no Kimi''' (廬原君), led 10,000 soldiers to help [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] in 662. Personal name unknown. * {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|WD=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province|1=Tsukushi Province}} (筑紫国) ** '''{{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Tsukushi no Kimi Iwai|1=Iwai Rebellion}}''' (筑紫君磐井, died 528), political arch-rival of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} and led a failed rebellion in 527, known as the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Iwai Rebellion|1=Iwai Rebellion}} ending with his death. The Iwatoyama kofun in {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka|1=Fukuoka}} was recorded in ''Fudoki of Chikugo'' to be his tomb. ** '''Tsukushi no Kimi Kazuko''' (筑紫君葛子), son of Iwai. He feared being put to death on implication because of his father's rebellion, and gave to the Emperor the ''miyake'' of Kasuya. There is no record of his death, so it is believed he lived on. ** '''Tsukushi no Kimi Sachiyama''' (筑紫君薩野馬 / 筑紫君薩夜麻), in 671 he served as an envoy for {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} and in 690 for [[:en:Empress Jito|Empress Jito]] with the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty|1=Tang dynasty}} of {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China|1=China}}. * {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}} (Yamashiro Province ) ** '''Miwa no Kimi Sakau''' (三輪君逆, died 586), brother of Osazaki. He was minister for [[Emperor Bidatsu]]. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Osazaki''' (三輪君小鷦鷯), brother of Sakau. He suffered intense questioning for raping the ladies in waiting of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}} and stabbed himself in the neck and died. ** '''Miwa no Kurukuma no Kimi Azumahito''' (三輪栗隈君東人), from a cadet branch of the Miwa clan. In 645 he was dispatched two times to confirm the border between [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] and the [[:en:Gaya confederacy|Gaya confederacy]]. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Okuchi''' (輪君大口), from a cadet branch of the Miwa clan. Lived during the reign of [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Fumiya''' (三輪文屋, died 643), son of Osazaki. He escaped with {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|en|Prince Yamashiro|WD=Q3137695|lt=Prince Yamashiro|1=Prince Yamashiro}}, the son of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}} to {{ill|Mount Ikoma|en|Mount Ikoma|WD=Q996259|lt=Mount Ikoma|1=Mount Ikoma}} (生駒山) and they were both killed there. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Shikobu''' (三輪君色夫), son of Sakau. He was dispatched with the army to {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}} in 649. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Nemaro''' (三輪君根麻呂), general who was dispatched to Japan with 27,000 soldiers in 663 and fought the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|en|Battle of Baekgang|WD=Q608727|lt=Battle of Baekgang|1=Battle of Baekgang}}. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Togane''' (三輪君利金), son of Fumiya, father of Takechimaro. He was Ranked Daikajō (大花上), which was a high crown Rank. The Ōmiwa clan regained power during his generation. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Kobito''' (三輪君子首, died 676), son of Shikobu. He fought for {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}.

Kimi Takami''' (猪名公高見, died 672), lived during the time of [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. * {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}} (近江国) ** '''Inukami no Kimi Mitasuki''' (犬上君三田耜 / 犬上御田鍬), sent by {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} in 614 to {{ill|Sui dynasty|en|Sui dynasty|WD=Q7405|lt=Sui dynasty|1=Sui dynasty}}, {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China|1=China}} returning home 615 from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]]. Also sent by {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}} as an envoy to the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty|1=Tang dynasty}}, {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China|1=China}} in 630. ** '''Inukami no Kimi Takerube''' (犬上君健部), minister of [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. ** '''Inukami no Kimi Shiromaro''' (犬上君白麻呂), son of Mitasuki, sent as ambassador by [[:en:Empress Saimei|Empress Saimei]] to {{ill|Goguryeo|en|Goguryeo|WD=Q28370|lt=Goguryeo|1=Goguryeo}}. * {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}} (下野国) ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Okuma''' (上毛野君小熊), participated in the feud of the [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}, Omi and his relative Oki. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Katana''' (上毛野君形名), father of Wakako. In 637 he led an expedition against the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}}. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Wakako''' (上毛野君稚子), led 27,000 soldiers to help [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] in 662. ** '''Kamitsukeno no Kimi Michiji''' (上毛野君三千), son of Wakako, government official. * {{ill|Suruga Province|en|Suruga Province|WD=Q864170|lt=Suruga Province|1=Suruga Province}} (駿河国) ** '''Iohara no Kimi''' (廬原君), led 10,000 soldiers to help [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] in 662. Personal name unknown. * {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|WD=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province|1=Tsukushi Province}} (筑紫国) ** '''{{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Tsukushi no Kimi Iwai|1=Iwai Rebellion}}''' (筑紫君磐井, died 528), political arch-rival of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} and led a failed rebellion in 527, known as the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Iwai Rebellion|1=Iwai Rebellion}} ending with his death. The Iwatoyama kofun in {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka|1=Fukuoka}} was recorded in ''Fudoki of Chikugo'' to be his tomb. ** '''Tsukushi no Kimi Kazuko''' (筑紫君葛子), son of Iwai. He feared being put to death on implication because of his father's rebellion, and gave to the Emperor the ''miyake'' of Kasuya. There is no record of his death, so it is believed he lived on. ** '''Tsukushi no Kimi Sachiyama''' (筑紫君薩野馬 / 筑紫君薩夜麻), in 671 he served as an envoy for {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} and in 690 for [[:en:Empress Jito|Empress Jito]] with the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty|1=Tang dynasty}} of {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China|1=China}}. * {{ill|Yamashiro Province|en|Yamashiro Province|WD=Q749276|lt=Yamashiro Province|1=Yamashiro Province}} (Yamashiro Province ) ** '''Miwa no Kimi Sakau''' (三輪君逆, died 586), brother of Osazaki. He was minister for [[Emperor Bidatsu]]. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Osazaki''' (三輪君小鷦鷯), brother of Sakau. He suffered intense questioning for raping the ladies in waiting of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|WD=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}} and stabbed himself in the neck and died. ** '''Miwa no Kurukuma no Kimi Azumahito''' (三輪栗隈君東人), from a cadet branch of the Miwa clan. In 645 he was dispatched two times to confirm the border between [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] and the [[:en:Gaya confederacy|Gaya confederacy]]. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Okuchi''' (輪君大口), from a cadet branch of the Miwa clan. Lived during the reign of [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Fumiya''' (三輪文屋, died 643), son of Osazaki. He escaped with {{ill|Prince Yamashiro|en|Prince Yamashiro|WD=Q3137695|lt=Prince Yamashiro|1=Prince Yamashiro}}, the son of {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|WD=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}} to {{ill|Mount Ikoma|en|Mount Ikoma|WD=Q996259|lt=Mount Ikoma|1=Mount Ikoma}} (生駒山) and they were both killed there. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Shikobu''' (三輪君色夫), son of Sakau. He was dispatched with the army to {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}} in 649. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Nemaro''' (三輪君根麻呂), general who was dispatched to Japan with 27,000 soldiers in 663 and fought the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|en|Battle of Baekgang|WD=Q608727|lt=Battle of Baekgang|1=Battle of Baekgang}}. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Togane''' (三輪君利金), son of Fumiya, father of Takechimaro. He was Ranked Daikajō (大花上), which was a high crown Rank. The Ōmiwa clan regained power during his generation. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Kobito''' (三輪君子首, died 676), son of Shikobu. He was minister of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}. ** '''Miwa no Kimi Takechimaro''' (三輪君高市麻呂, 657–706), son of Togane, grandson of Fumiya. He fought for {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}.

{{wikidata link|Q39078946}} [[Category:Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Japanese words and phrases]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 100+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 150+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 200+ untranslated japanese characters]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kimi (kabane)]][[ja:キミ (カバネ)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kimi (kabane)]][[ja:キミ (カバネ)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kimi (kabane)]][[ja:キミ (カバネ)]]
{{Short description|Ancient Japanese hereditary title}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}} {{Italic title|reason=
}} {{nihongo|'''''Kimi'''''|君}} was an ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}ese hereditary title denoting Rank and political standing (a ''[[kabane]]'') that was reserved for certain members of the ''Tomo no [[Kuni no miyatsuko|Miyatsuko]]'' clans, which were clans associated with particular occupations. They became a {{ill|Hereditary title|en|Hereditary title|qid=Q5737899|lt=Hereditary title|1=Hereditary title}} passed down the generations. The ''kimi'' Rank was lower than the ''[[muraji]]'' and ''[[Omi (title)|omi]]'' Ranks in political power and standing during much of the [[Kofun period]] and {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} which are collectively grouped as the {{ill|Yamato period|en|Yamato period|qid=Q633881|lt=Yamato period|1=Yamato period}}.<ref>Nihon Shoki</ref><ref>Shinsen Shōjiroku</ref>
"''Kimi''" literally means "Lord" (of a local area). For example, in the name, Kamitsukeno no Kimi Wakako, "Kamitsukeno no kimi" means "Lord of Kamitsukeno" and Wakako is his personal name. Sometimes the character (公) is used in place of (君).<ref name="Clans and Religion in Ancient Japan">{{cite book|last1=Suzuki|first1=Masanobu|title=Clans and Religion in Ancient Japan: The mythology of Mt. Miwa|date=2016|publisher=Taylor & Francis|location=Routledge|isbn=9781317209348|pages=170|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=h0InDAAAQBAJ|accessdate=1 August 2017}}</ref>
==List of ''Kimi'' (君)== ** '''Oka no Kimi Yoroshi''' (岡君宜), sent as envoy to {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|qid=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty|1=Tang dynasty}}, {{ill|China|en|China|qid=Q148|lt=China|1=China}} for [[Emperor Kōtoku]]. * {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|qid=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province|1=Bungo Province}} (豊後国) ** '''Okita no Kimi Esaka''' (大分君惠尺, died 675), important member of the court for {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|qid=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|qid=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}. ** '''Okita no Kimi Wakaomi''' (大分君稚臣, died 679) also called '''Wakami''' (稚見), important member of the court for {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|qid=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|qid=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}. * {{ill|Hi Province|en|Hi Province|qid=Q1156510|lt=Hi Province|1=Hi Province}} (火国 or 肥国) ** '''Hi no Kimi Take Wokumi''' (火君健男組 / 肥君健男組), legendary ancestor of the Hi no Kimi clan who was granted the title by {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}} for pacifying the area. ** '''Hi no Kimi Ite''' (火君猪手 / 肥君猪手), lived in northern Kyushu in 702 during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Monmu|en|Emperor Monmu|qid=Q357246|lt=Emperor Monmu|1=Emperor Monmu}}. * {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|qid=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke Province|1=Kōzuke Province}} (上野国) ** '''Sami no Sukunamaro''' (佐味宿那麻呂 / 佐味君少麻呂), fought for [[:en:Emperor Temmu|Emperor Temmu]] during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|qid=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}. * {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} who sent him after in 665 to the {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|qid=Q9683|lt=Tang dynasty|1=Tang dynasty}} as an envoy. ** '''Mori no Kimi
